name: Unreal Engine Project

topDirs.Binaries: Generated build output; do not edit manually.
topDirs.Intermediate: Generated build intermediates; do not edit manually.
topDirs.Saved: Local/editor generated state; do not edit manually.
topDirs.DerivedDataCache: Generated asset cache; do not edit manually.
topDirs.TypeScript/Typing: Generated Unreal TypeScript declaration output; do not edit manually.
topDirs.SoundBanks: Audio middleware generated assets, often Wwise.

snapshot.overview: This appears to be an Unreal Engine project.
invariants: Do not edit Binaries, Intermediate, Saved, or DerivedDataCache unless explicitly requested.
Prefer source, config, or plugin edits over generated output.
Asset references may be path-sensitive; validate renames through Unreal-aware tooling.
conventions: Treat *.Build.cs and *.Target.cs as module/build entry points.

riskHints: Do not edit generated Unreal output unless explicitly requested.

architecture: Unreal project manifests define enabled plugins, module visibility, and startup surfaces before source files are considered.
Runtime modules should own game/client runtime behavior; editor modules should own editor tooling and asset-authoring workflows.
C++ reflection declarations are source-of-truth for UnrealHeaderTool and downstream binding generators.
Config and Content paths may be consumed indirectly through Unreal loaders, soft references, Blueprints, and generated metadata.
beforeEditWorkflow: Run a before-edit project graph query for the target file, module, or subsystem.
Run an impact project graph query for the same target before changing generated files, assets, build files, plugins, configs, or bindings.
Read the exact files to be edited and search for direct callers, generated outputs, configs, and TypeScript consumers when the change crosses boundaries.
State the affected chain before editing when C++/TypeScript/plugin/config/assets boundaries are involved.
If the affected chain includes generated files, assets, build files, plugins, or TypeScript bindings, identify the source of truth before editing.
criticalInvariants: TypeScript/Typing is generated Unreal TypeScript declaration output and must not be edited manually.
Binaries, Intermediate, Saved, and DerivedDataCache are generated or local output areas and must not be treated as source.
Changing .uplugin or *.Build.cs requires checking plugin dependency declarations and module dependency direction together.
Runtime modules must not depend on editor-only modules.
Content asset paths may be used by soft references; do not treat ordinary file renames as safe without Unreal-aware validation.
Generated declarations must be driven by C++ reflection source or the binding generator, not by hand-edited generated output.
.uproject plugin enable/disable changes are high-impact and require impact analysis before editing.
conventions: Treat .uproject as the project-level manifest and plugin enablement surface.
Treat .uplugin files as plugin module boundary and load-phase declarations.
Treat *.Build.cs as module dependency declarations, not ordinary C# application code.
Prefer project graph system pages and playbooks before raw node pages when planning non-trivial edits.

moduleResponsibilities.role: Primary runtime module for project entry, target integration, and foundational runtime dependencies.
editingRules: Check the owning *.Build.cs before adding module dependencies.
Do not add editor-only dependencies to runtime modules.

moduleResponsibilities.role: Editor-only tooling, asset editors, importers, and workflow extensions.
moduleResponsibilities.runtimeImpact: Should not become a dependency of runtime modules.
editingRules: Keep editor-only APIs out of runtime dependency chains.

moduleResponsibilities.role: Plugin runtime behavior used by game/client modules.
editingRules: Validate runtime/editor boundary when changing dependencies or load phases.

moduleResponsibilities.role: Generated TypeScript declarations for Unreal-exposed native APIs.
editingRules: Do not edit generated typings manually.
Change C++ reflection source or the generator/configuration instead.

flows.name: Native C++ API exposed to TypeScript
steps: C++ reflection declarations define the native API surface.
The binding generator consumes reflection metadata and generator configuration.
Generated TypeScript declarations are written under TypeScript/Typing.
TypeScript business code consumes generated declarations.

flows.name: Plugin dependency and module load flow
steps: .uplugin declares plugin modules, module type, loading phase, and plugin dependencies.
Runtime/editor targets load compatible modules based on type and phase.
validation: Check .uplugin dependencies against Build.cs module dependencies.
Check runtime modules do not depend on editor-only modules.
Validate editor/runtime startup for changed plugins.

flows.name: Asset reference flow
steps: Content assets may be referenced by object path from C++, config, Blueprints, data assets, or generated metadata.
Soft references may not appear as direct source imports.
Renames and moves require Unreal-aware reference validation.
validation: Use Unreal-aware asset reference checks before and after path changes.

playbooks.changeType: Modify C++ reflection API consumed by TypeScript
beforeEdit: Query project graph neighbors for the target type.
Check whether TypeScript/Typing has a generated declaration.
edit: Modify C++ source/header or generator configuration.
verify: Run type generation or confirm generated output path.

beforeEdit: Check plugin dependencies, module type, loading phase, public/private dependency direction, and runtime/editor boundary.
edit: Keep plugin dependency declarations and module dependencies consistent.
verify: Build affected target and validate startup where possible.

beforeEdit: Query asset references and impact before renaming or moving assets.
edit: Use Unreal-aware rename/move tooling when available.

validationCommands.name: UBT compile
validationCommands.note: Use the project-approved UnrealBuildTool command for the affected target.

validationCommands.name: TypeScript declaration generation
validationCommands.note: Run the project-approved binding/type generation command, or mark it as maintainer-confirmed if not automated.

validationCommands.name: TypeScript validation
validationCommands.note: Run the project-approved TypeScript typecheck/build command for consumers.

validationCommands.name: Plugin dependency consistency
validationCommands.note: Run Mindstrate project graph plugin dependency and module boundary checks.

validationCommands.name: Asset reference validation
validationCommands.note: Use Unreal-aware reference validation for asset renames or moves.

suggestedSystemPages.title: Gameplay Loop
knownConstraints: TODO: pin the actual gameplay loop entry (e.g. AGameModeBase / UGameInstance subclass) once confirmed.
doNotEditTargets: TODO: list any auto-generated state-machine code or generated cue tables that must be regenerated rather than hand-edited.
suggestedSystemPages.affectedChain: AGameModeBase / UGameInstance subclass -> player controller -> pawn / character -> per-system component activations.
sourceOfTruth: TODO: name the canonical AGameModeBase / UGameInstance subclass header in Source/.
recommendedVerification: Run the project-approved PIE / standalone smoke test for the gameplay loop after any change.

suggestedSystemPages.title: Network Replication
knownConstraints: Replicated UPROPERTY changes must be reviewed against bandwidth/relevancy or they regress at scale.
Server RPC ordering vs. multicast vs. client RPC must be deliberate; do not flip without re-validating authority assumptions.
doNotEditTargets: TODO: list any frozen wire-format or cooked-build replication payloads that cannot be hand-edited.
suggestedSystemPages.affectedChain: Replicated UPROPERTY / RPC declaration -> reflection -> dedicated/listen server -> client mispredict / rollback path.
sourceOfTruth: TODO: name the authoritative replication contract location (e.g. Source/<Project>/Public/Net/<File>.h).
recommendedVerification: Run a multi-client PIE network smoke test with at least 2 clients.
Inspect the network profiler trace for the affected actor.

suggestedSystemPages.title: Asset Loading
knownConstraints: Async asset loads must go through UAssetManager so primary asset lifetimes are consistent.
Soft references and DataAsset rows are path-sensitive; renames must use Unreal-aware reference validation.
doNotEditTargets: TODO: list any cooked manifest or chunk layout file that must be regenerated rather than edited.
suggestedSystemPages.affectedChain: DefaultGame.ini AssetManagerSettings -> UAssetManager -> primary asset class -> streaming / chunk / cook layout -> runtime load.
sourceOfTruth: Config/DefaultGame.ini AssetManagerSettings, plus the project's UAssetManager subclass.
recommendedVerification: Run the project-approved cook / chunk validation command after changing primary asset rules.
Run an in-PIE async load smoke test.

suggestedSystemPages.title: Gameplay Ability System (GAS)
knownConstraints: GameplayAttributeSet members and GameplayEffect modifiers form the data contract; do not refactor without auditing every consumer.
GameplayTags must be defined in INI / DataTable; do not hardcode tag strings in C++ that diverge from the central registry.
doNotEditTargets: TODO: list any generated attribute getters/setters (often produced by GAS macros) that must come from the macro expansion rather than hand edits.
suggestedSystemPages.affectedChain: UAttributeSet declaration -> GameplayEffect / GameplayAbility -> AbilitySystemComponent on the avatar -> Blueprint widget bindings -> network replication.
sourceOfTruth: TODO: name the central GameplayTags registry and the canonical UAttributeSet header location.
recommendedVerification: Run combat / ability smoke tests in PIE.
Check GAS replication on a 2-client network PIE session if the change is replicated.

suggestedSystemPages.title: UI (UMG / Slate)
knownConstraints: UMG widget asset paths are referenced from C++ via TSoftClassPtr / class redirectors; renames must use Unreal-aware tooling.
Localization keys are part of the UI contract; changing a key without updating translation tables breaks localized builds.
doNotEditTargets: TODO: list any generated UMG bindings or auto-generated text catalog files that must be regenerated.
suggestedSystemPages.affectedChain: UMG widget asset -> WidgetTree binding -> ViewModel / controller -> gameplay-side data source.
sourceOfTruth: TODO: name the project's UMG / ViewModel base classes and the localization manifest path.
recommendedVerification: Run UMG smoke pass in PIE.
Run localization gather/cook check after changing visible strings.

suggestedSystemPages.title: Config and Tuning
knownConstraints: Config values flow through Engine/Game/Editor INI hierarchy; setting a value in the wrong layer can be silently overridden.
Hot-loaded config values must be subscribed via FCoreDelegates / OnConfigSectionsChanged; do not assume one-shot reads.
doNotEditTargets: TODO: list any baked or cooked config artifact that should not be edited directly.
suggestedSystemPages.affectedChain: Config/Default*.ini -> CDO defaults -> runtime read sites -> live tuning subscribers.
sourceOfTruth: Config/Default*.ini for the affected category, plus the C++ class that reads it.
recommendedVerification: Run the project-approved config validation step.
Run the gameplay smoke test that exercises the tuned subsystem.
